rxn_class_summary: Visible-light/photosensitizer-induced dearomative [5+2] (and divergent [2+2], interrupted [5+2], [5+4]) cycloaddition of indole/pyrrole-tethered vinylcyclopropanes (VCPs) via energy-transfer sensitization to triplet biradicals.

key_intermediates.name: Triplet excited indole-tethered VCP (1a-T1) generated by energy transfer from Ir photosensitizer
Triplet 1,4-biradical INT1a-T1/INT1b-T1 (C2-C/β-C bond formed, radicals at C3 of indoline and cyclopropane carbon)
Triplet 1,7-biradical INT2-E-T1 / INT2-Z-T1 (after cyclopropane ring-opening, E or Z olefin)
Open-shell singlet 1,4-biradical INT1a-OSS / INT1b-OSS (after ISC via MECPs)
Open-shell singlet 1,7-biradical INT2-Z-OSS (productive for [5+2] ring closure via TS3)
Open-shell singlet 1,7-biradical INT2-E-OSS (unreactive toward [5+2]; routed to interrupted [5+2] via zwitterion)
Zwitterionic intermediate (indolyl cation / malonate anion) formed from E-1,7-biradical with HFIP/H2O as proton shuttle

electron_flow: Visible-light excitation of the Ir photosensitizer to its triplet state transfers energy (Dexter, not SET) to the indole core of the substrate, generating a triplet indole; intramolecular C2–Cβ bond formation furnishes a triplet 1,4-biradical that undergoes cyclopropane ring-opening to a triplet 1,7-biradical, which crosses to the open-shell singlet via MECPs; the Z-1,7-biradical recombines to the [5+2] product, while the E-1,7-biradical (in HFIP) is protonated/deprotonated through a zwitterion to give the interrupted [5+2] product, and C3-substituted substrates close instead to [2+2] cyclobutane-fused indolines.
rate_determining_step: Intersystem crossing / ring-opening through TS2a-T1 or TS2b-T1 (~35–38 kcal/mol) connecting the triplet 1,4- to 1,7-biradicals; final C–C ring closure via TS3 (32.0 kcal/mol) on the OSS surface for [5+2].

probe_reagents.name: 2,5-dimethylhexa-2,4-diene (triplet quencher)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction largely inhibited, supporting involvement of triplet intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: Stern–Volmer quenching with substrate 1a vs indoline analogue 1a'
probe_reagents.observation: Excited PS is quenched by indole-tethered 1a but not by the indoline analogue 1a', consistent with energy transfer to the indole chromophore.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a
probe_reagents.observation: Redox potentials of 1a do not match those of the photosensitizer, excluding a photoredox SET pathway.

probe_reagents.name: HFIP-d2 (deuterated solvent)
probe_reagents.observation: 63% D incorporation at the malonate α-position of 6a-d, supporting a zwitterionic intermediate with HFIP as proton shuttle.

probe_reagents.name: HFIP/D2O (5:1)
probe_reagents.observation: 42% D incorporation in 6b-d, consistent with proton transfer through a zwitterion.

probe_reagents.name: Sc(OTf)3 additive (for 7-azaindole substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: Stoichiometric Sc(OTf)3 restores reasonable yield (73%) of 2ad by bidentate coordination to the 7-azaindole.

probe_reagents.name: DFT calculations [(U)B3LYP-D3(BJ)/def2-TZVP//def2-SVP]
probe_reagents.observation: Map triplet and OSS biradical surfaces, MECPs, and TS energies; rationalize [5+2] vs [2+2] vs interrupted [5+2] divergence.

notes: Mechanism is energy-transfer (Dexter) photosensitization, explicitly distinguished from photoredox SET (CV mismatch, Stern–Volmer with indoline analogue). Divergent pathway selection is governed by the fate of the open-shell singlet 1,7-biradical: Z-isomer → [5+2]; E-isomer → interrupted [5+2] via zwitterion (favored in HFIP); C3-substituted indoles → [2+2] (via INT1-OSS ring closure through TS4a/b); pyrrole-tethered VCPs switch to [5+4] cycloaddition with C5 of pyrrole. Direct [2+2] from INT1a/b-OSS is a competitive but thermodynamically disfavored pathway for indole substrates (PRO-2+2a/b only −5.6/−5.2 vs 2a at −21.2 kcal/mol).
